Not as good as the first one.

As of writing this, I have beaten the game once.

Things to note before considering this game:

Firstly, as many, many others have pointed out, this game attempts to (and stumbles at) mixing the Salt & Sanctuary style with monster hunter style gameplay. This causes a weird situation where the limited content of the game becomes increasingly obvious the more you play it (Something the previous game hid well, with its alchemy system to transmute weapons and such.) The problem stems from all your craftables being in one menu, rather than being on a per item basis, which lets you see just how few equipment options (craftables) there are. Combined with the tendency that I assume most have to use one set of equipment until you find something sutibly better, you end up using maybe 5% of anything you find, which makes wanting to hunt more mages a... Questionable... Endeavor.

Secondly: Due to the Monster Hunter "locale" style of maps, the world starts feeling small, and sometimes labyrinth like... And not in the fun way. Most traversal tools see VERY limited usage in previous locales, whereas the later locales do a good job at asking you to use all of your traversal tools. It left me feeling betrayed coming back to the first locale because all I could say after doing the short multi traversal section was "that's it? That's all I get for coming back here?" This, tragically, makes it to where you're strongly encouraged NOT to backtrack outside of some limited things.

With that wordy preface out of the way, let's get into the meat of things:

Does the combat feel good? 3-7/10 depending on the boss/enemy
Sometimes yes, sometimes no. Some of the most fun I've had with the combat was against like 3 of the mob enemies, who do a good job at encouraging you to use a good mix of perfect blocking, dodging, and attacking. But other than those, it can feel very "kill them before they can kill you" at times. Especially when just about everything threatens to send you flying (and plummeting) to your death. The bosses, well... Overall decent, but tends to be very much a game of "run away until all the death puddles/orbs/pokies/etc. go away, then maybe consider attacking, if the boss isn't already lunging at you with its probably 3 swing attack sequence, then get a couple pokes in, and repeat. Some bosses can be especially infuriating, while others made me think that it was too easy. All in all, the combat can be a bit inconsistent because of the selection of enemies there are, and the fact that sometimes the game forgets that it's not dragon ball, and no, you cannot actually move and react at mach 2 through 52 different projectiles.

Level design: 6/10
Pretty solid, just limit the number of times you're returning to a previous area as much as possible once you have the next area's traversal tool. Finding stuff can be tedious, due to the lack of a map mechanic. You aren't missing much, trust me.

Visuals: 9/10
Looks just about as rad as Salt & Sanctuary. If just a tiny bit different.

Overall... 6.7/10
It just barely passes for my recommendation. Solid idea that ended up being half baked. Also brought down by the expectations of the I.P.

If you plan on trying it, I would say it's a good idea to wait for a sale.

Overall I still look forward to their next game.

和boss法术对轰，玩得挺上头的。不过割裂的战斗体验和崩坏的数值让人不爽。软上限设的太低了，伤害从中期就基本没有太多提升。一周目法师血太少了，后面伤害却高的离谱，打法师基本就是法术对轰互秒。猎杀法师的设定一开始还算新奇，但是后面地图恶意的设计，小怪超高的伤害，追法师实在折磨。狩猎过的法师会到处跑，开图的时候来几只堆在一起实在折磨，但是后面想刷装备又找不到对应法师在哪，没刷命定的话基本只能硬找，但是不同地图的法师又会到处跑，实在是太难找特定的额哪个法师了。关底boss血又忽然厚的离谱，伤害也是高的不像话，但是演出却着实不咋地。武器和加点的设计也割裂，信仰的镰刀中五把有两把不吃信仰，两把额外吃敏捷，两把额外吃力量，最后的五级镰刀力吃敏又不吃信仰，要用非信仰系镰刀的战技又要另外加奥术法术。而且有把吃敏捷的镰刀攻速反而攻速慢耗精多，其他吃力量的镰刀反而攻速比它快。这就导致一把前期到手的两级镰刀一直用到结束。其他镰刀也都做过了，但是加点跟不上，想从开局的两级镰刀玩到最后的五级镰刀一共要加四种点，另外还有三个职业要求。如此诸多不合理的设计实在给不了好评。

Пожалуй начать хочется с того что если вы ищете продолжение Salt and Sanctuary то вам не сюда. Единственное сходство этих игр в стилистике,во всем остальном Sacrifice либо слишком отличается, либо банально хуже.
Итак по порядку:
Геймплей - представляет собой бесконечный фарм +/- одинаковых боссов для крафта новой брони и оружия, аля Monster Hunter, уже к 10 часу начало душить.
Сюжет - ну... он есть. Очередной пепельный который должен возжечь пламя для спасения страдающего мира, ничего нового.
Локации - по факту представляют собой небольшие зацикленные"крендельки" или "узлы" по которым придется неустанно бегать ради фарма, они разнообразны но не шибко большие что довольно печально.
Визуал - как и писал выше, игры по стилистике похожи, но Sacrifice стал чуть более 3Дшным что-ли, как по мне не очень удачное решение.
Враги - в игре изобилуют всевозможные "хвататели", "сбиватели с ног" и прочая раздражающая хрень которая в купе с неуклюжестью физики персонажа приводит к ваншотам + ко всему кривые хит боксы, атаки сквозь стены, захваты в полете, често говоря складывается впечатление что разработчики специально сделали все так чтобы побесить игрока - неприятно.

По итогу - пройти конечно можно, но я бы не советовал, игра лютый средняк, лучше перепройти Salt and Sanctuary.

[h1]以下是关于盐系列的问题与解答[/h1]不想看的可以直接跳到Q5
[h3]Q1.为什么盐与避难所比盐与献祭更加有趣？[/h3]
就说一个很多盐1玩家都印象深刻的一个场景：最终boss战前的神殿，BGM所渲染的氛围使人起鸡皮疙瘩，而令人印象深刻的便是最终boss无名之神，以及打败无名之神后的落井，无论是画面特效还是音乐氛围都拉的满满的，让人难以忘怀。
盐1的地图设计不说顶尖，至少每个场景上各有各的特色，虽然有些地图特别恶心，但这也是给予玩家印象深刻的条件之一。
而盐2，不得不说，盐2的图真的做到了没有特点全是答辩，学魂都学不会非要学魂1那种只能在固定篝火之间传送，但凡加个在篝火处随时升级也不至于被诟病的这么严重。
玩过盐2的人都能感觉出来，盐2比起魂，更像是怪猎(法师换区、素材获取)、仁王(各种猎奇怪)、只狼(抓钩、格挡和完美格挡)、法环(数不胜数的武器战技、材料收集)的结合体，而且做的这些机制根本就难以往好的方向去评价，学怪猎的换区结果让法师到处跑把玩家当狗一样遛，学其它游戏的格挡+完美格挡结果格挡收益低的离谱甚至没有，学魂的捷径和内味结果一个都没做到只学到了诟病最多的魂1固定式传送。相当于把坏的都缝了好的都丢了。
而盐2的这种“创新”，让只喜欢游玩类魂的玩家在玩了盐2后对比盐1有着非常之大的反差感，对盐2的评价也将随之变更。
[h3]Q2.为什么说相比于盐与献祭，盐与避难所更好玩且有重玩价值？[/h3]
盐1的地图连通性极强，有类魂游戏独有的捷径、创新的七大教派、独特的boss，和盐专属的特色道具——烙印(拥有各种效果的不同烙印，玩家可以利用相应的烙印前往前中后期原本无法踏足的地方)，再加上令人感到新奇的新鲜设定烛台(亮起的蜡烛代表其他玩家的初见boss通过率)、处刑架(上面挂着不久前在这片区域死去的玩家)，以及地图探索奖励(隐藏门、盐袋盐盒盐箱、不同属性的武器护符、各种特色戒指等)、类魂游戏特有的轮椅(最经典的黄金酒大剪刀)，这些全都魂味满满。
至于盐1，或者说类魂游戏的重玩价值......不太好说，毕竟每个重玩的玩家都有自己的想法，像是提高难度挑战自身、不想开新号而是选择多开周目去拿白金成就之类的。
反观盐2，盐与避难所所展现的bgm氛围与画面特效那是一个都没做到，没有一个地方能够让人印象深刻，初见未竟的献祭我甚至都没意识到他竟然是最终boss。相比于盐1的最终boss无名之神，盐2的最终boss未竟的献祭开场时缓动画甚至是四肢伏地跪在地上爬起来，这b格也太低了吧。
而且盐2与盐1不同，盐1是正经类魂，是一个巨大的箱庭世界，而盐2则把类魂游戏最有内味的箱庭世界分成了多张地图，也不是说这样做不好，但是无论如何，的确有很多玩家觉得这样的多图箱庭确实没内味。真的不如改成这张图的最右边能够去到下张图的最左边，再把图与图之间的地理位置调整一下，不加入盐1那种无意义的近路，那指定是个好箱庭。
再就是盐2的重玩价值。盐2若要开启新周目，就代表着你又要重复一遍一周目中一模一样的法师追逐战。
10成时间里有5成时间跑图找路，3成时间清法师小怪，2成时间法师终于不跑了你给他打死。盐2的核心就是靠杀冠名法师推动主线，而其中还有无名法师和命定法师，你每想杀一只法师，追逐战的这些步骤你就得重复一次，你就说有没有重玩价值吧。
更重要的是，盐2他并不像魂环那样每一周目提升一点难度，而是在二周目开始，敌人的数值就直接提升好几个档次，这对只想取中间值难度的玩家非常不友好。
[h3]Q3.为什么盐与献祭的游玩人数如此之低？[/h3]
一款好的游戏，除了专属于这款游戏的创新和独特玩法，更重要的是参考其它游戏并吸取其精华。而盐2则完美做到了弃精华之所在，取百家之糟粕。
无数人不认可盐2不仅仅是盐2的创新不够精妙，更是盐2刚开始发售时令人想砸电脑的烂优化，再加上盐2发售不久后播放量火速到达几十万的盐2避雷视频，以及当时已经体验过盐2洗礼进而劝告他人不要购买盐2的这些事情，即使当时的ska进行了加紧的持续优化，但这根本就是亡羊补牢，因此还是导致了现如今的盐2无人问津。
[h3]Q4.为什么盐与献祭的评价与购买人数都很低，可还是有人乐此不疲的在玩？[/h3]
每个人都有自己喜欢的游戏，每个游戏都有自己的忠实玩家，盐2当然也不例外。
[h3]Q5.现在的盐与献祭值得入坑吗？[/h3]
除了玩家PVP之间的相互秒人外，游戏到现在已经优化的中规中矩了，可以一试。
对于我这种喜欢刷刷刷且喜欢探索宝藏，或是想跟另一个朋友一块儿无缝联机游玩的玩家来说，值得入坑。
对于喜欢开荒的感觉，或是想当太阳去帮人以及当坏东西入侵然后相互秒人的玩家来说，酌情入坑。
对于不喜欢经常需要跑图，或是只想体验原汁原味的类魂游戏玩家来说，不值得入坑。

This game is a genre-blend of Salt and Sanctuary, Monster Hunter, and Dark Souls covenant features that unfortunately didn't stick the landing. While the attempt to create an entry built around multiplayer functionality is admirable, their implementation of the Monster Hunter features clash with the soulslike design. For instance, map areas serve the purpose of hunting locales rather than as dungeon crawls, with the ease of traversal in the zones forcing the navigation and encounter design to be watered down to accommodate the change. Any loot that is hand placed around the map will at best be the starting equipment of another class or the most basic form of a weapon, as the vast majority of equipment has been placed behind the mage hunt mechanic. Another example would be instead of finding or purchasing consumables, they can crafted when you rest at a checkpoint with materials which can gathered throughout the locale or looted from trash mobs. While maintaining a steady supply of these materials is simple (and admittedly preferable to them being an inconvenience), it begs the question as to why they had to be limited in quantity to begin with, other than to be in parody with Monster Hunter. 

The "Mage Hunts" which are the main form of PvE content fail to remain novel unlike their Monster Hunter counterparts due to the overuse of the same boss designs and lack of interactivity failing to encourage innovative approaches. The hunts themselves boil down to going on a wild goose chase as you pursue the mage while they spawn trash mobs before teleporting away in a scripted sequence, at most only throwing an attack or two at each skirmish. After the mage reaches half health or they reach their final location, the actual boss fight begins, which usually takes less than one minute to complete. The experience of these hunts are deflating, as the pace is too fast to take in and the difficulty just as vacant. To add salt to the wound, instead of being able to start a hunt for them at the lobby at any time, after the first hunt, a mage must be located in their respective locale at random, with a roaming pattern for you to memorize at best. And even after all the time spend grinding, most equipment that can be made from mage parts don't meaningfully change your playstyle, usually only buffing damage numbers or defense to a certain element or two or adding a generic effect to an action like perfect blocking or killing an enemy. 

And if you were wondering about boss variety, this game will be sure to disappoint. When they say each hunt is against a mage, they literally mean it. Each mage has a set of three to four trash mobs to summon during the chase which generally follow the same archetypes throughout, a main weapon that they make basic melee swings with, and have about three spell attacks that are either projectiles or AoEs which they can use in quick succession. If it weren't for their lack of health, each mage would be frustrating to fight as there are rarely if ever clear openings to strike back and attacks wide enough that they'd hit both players regardless of the intended recipient. The only bosses that aren't mages are a boss that blocks the Metroidvania-style upgrades needed to unlock new modes of traversal and a boss that guards access to the next zone. Though, progressing through the game couldn't be that simple. There are locked doors that bar your progression through an area until you kill a required amount of mage types in that same area to progress. There are usually around four to six mages per area, and this doesn't include having to backtrack so you can grind those same mages for gear. While you only have to kill a couple of mages in each zone to gain access the next zone, this feature hampers the pace of exploration within each locale greatly, especially since there is no in-game map (and thus no map markers) to indicate places in a locale you haven't explored yet, even after you've put in the monotonous work of slaying the required mages. 

Worst of all, when I played this game with a friend, because of the earlier stated lack of interactivity, the experience didn't change in any meaningful way except for the ability to divide and conquer while exploring a locale, Though, I did appreciate the feature that when you do something in someone else's world that you didn't do in your own, the progress would sync up on your save too, as other multiplayer games often don't make this concession for progressing a game with a friend. Lastly, I was also unable to experience any of the Dark Souls multiplayer features such as being summoned to help with a mage hunt or invading another player's run due to the multiplayer community being non-existent at this point. To conclude, this game is not a Salt and Sanctuary sequel as advertised, it plays much more like a multiplayer spinoff. And even on its own terms, this game still falls flat as an experience due to the compromised level design, tedious mage bosses, and overall lack of meaningful progression. Even if someone adored the combat of Salt and Sanctuary or the hunts of Monster Hunter, chances are even those players would be beyond burnt out by the time the credits roll. 

My final consensus is that you best avoid this game.

Give it a shot, those Mixed reviews on Steam don't tell the whole story!

Salt and Sanctuary = 2d Dark Souls 
Salt and Sacrifice = 2d Monster Hunter

If its predecessor Salt & Sanctuary is like 2D Dark Souls, then Sacrifice is a little bit of Dark Souls mixed with Monster Hunter and Diablo due to the fact that online is largely the focus (something Sanctuary did not have at all). While the first game is more slow paced and story oriented, Sacrifice throws you right into the action and gives you many different ways to level. Getting to the end really isn't the goal here. You can level up through completing the story and going on nameless mage hunts, Daily hunts, and 6 different covenants. Multiplayer can be ignored entirely, but it will be a much slower grind because entering another player's world can easily climb you 10-20 levels in one session. If you don't like PvP there are a couple of PvE modes that are super fun. I highly recommend if you're looking for a fun and exciting action oriented version of Sanctuary.

There is a game to enjoy here for Souls-like fans.  You may want to consider investing in a trainer to help reach that enjoyment at a more reasonable pace.

This game is a blend of salt and sanctuary with a mix of monster hunter, what I mean by this is once you beat a named mage it becomes an enemy you can find and hunt down for their drops to craft weapons and armor out of.

The Good:
-The skill tree is a unique aspect that's enjoyable to go through.
-there's multiple play styles to choose melee/ranged/magic with multiple stat choices of: conviction, strength, dexterity, arcane, and/or luck.
-there's online co-op so you can play with your friend from start(once you reach the hub) to finish.

The Bad:
-mages clash often which is fun but not when you're doing a fated hunt or looking for a specific mage.
-pvp/multiplayer is mostly dead because of the low player base so if you're looking for random coop don't bother.
-the campaign is rather short.

Final Verdict:
It's enjoyable enough for me to keep playing it so if the good outweighs the bad for you get it on sale and enjoy it.

I deliberated quite a bit on whether I'd recommend this game or not. I was originally going to give it a thumbs up but with a big asterisk, but after thinking about it a bit more, what criticisms I would write about this game would make such a recommendation not make sense. So I'll start this with a preface;

I do enjoy this game. I have had fun just as much as I've had frustration, I would be willing to play this multiple times, and there are a number of elements that I prefer over Sacrifice than I did with Sanctuary. But as much as I want to give a positive review to ever so slightly displace this game the negative ones, Sacrifice has a lot of flaws, and the biggest issue it has is unfortunately very pronounced, rearing it's head prominently near the end of the 2nd world and all throughout the remaining content to various degrees, although you might get a taste of it as early as the very 1st world. And that issue is battle flow.

A lot of bosses and many late game enemies have completely ♥♥♥♥ flow. In most Souls-likes when non-fodder enemies attack, you usually have brief moments of respite in which you can get off your own, or that lets you back off easier to heal or reset the flow. Sanctuary had this most of the time. In Sacrifice? This courtesy is only extended for most of the 1st third, quarter or even fifth of the game. From there on out, enemies tend to have three features to them; most of their attacks fling you around, most of their attacks hit like trucks filled boulders, and most of their attack chains have 0 downtime between them in which you can attack or heal should you survive nearly being one-shot during a combo. There's other issues I personally dislike a fair bit, such as how annoying all the flying enemies are with most attacking you at range, having to always travel back to the hub to level up or buy stuff with no easy way to do so barring quit-outs, or how many areas have long falls that will result in very ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ deaths with how many things fling you around, but those don't compare to those very big issues.

And the endgame is filled with it, extending to even common field enemies. It is also not uncommon for multiple enemies to combo you into oblivion by flinging you about and giving 0 chance to roll to safety or heal in time.

I want to gush about the world-building and the visual style. I want to talk about all the genuinely hilarious moments where multiple roaming mages arrive and sic their minions on each-other, or squabble with field enemies hostile to them. I want to be able to compare Sacrifice to Sanctuary and say that it is an upgrade despite the simplified weapons, gear options and less open world, and to leave a positive recommendation. But I can't.

As of writing this, though I have yet to actually kill the final boss, I have killed all other bosses barring two, including those of the extra content. I have killed all the named mages, a couple of nameless and fated mages, and my current character's build is as done as it can be at about level 97. I fully intend to replay the game once I actually do get around to killing the final boss, and I do enjoy this game generally more than I did Sanctuary. But much like many others, I cannot recommend it.

I just hope the criticism of both Sanctuary and Sacrifice hasn't killed hope of a 3rd installment, and that I may leave a positive recommendation rather than a negative one.

I utterly loved the first game, Salt and Sanctuary. I 100%'d it, and even played multiple runs with different builds, something I usually never do in these type of games.

Salt and Sacrifice's game-play loop revolves around 2 main pillars:

1. Running around the map repeatedly grinding mages (mages are a type of boss).
2. Standard-issue moving from A -> B game-play.

The latter is fine. The former though, a complete chore to play through. Reading through the little discourse I could find on this game online, it appears that Monster Hunter has similar game-play and is clearly widely successful. When it's translated here though, it obviously does not work. Perhaps it's due to the inherent restriction of a 2D environment, or maybe it's due to the lack of mobility tools provided (It's not enough). Regardless of the reasoning, it's just boring and tedious.

The bosses and mobs in this game also seemingly have infinite stamina. They relentlessly unleash volleys upon volleys of attacks with apparently no cooldown. Having said that, this game is still surprisingly easy. I went with a 2-Handed play-through and 1-tried most bosses, and 2 tries on the few remaining. I just chugged flasks and spammed heavy attacks and it worked beautifully on almost everything, lol.

A complete slog to finish. I do appreciate the attempt at innovation though.

В итого - замкнутый круг, ходишь и получаешь люлей при этом не оказывая особого сопротивления. Вроде бы и интересно, и мир изучить и напряженно босса завалить, но пока ты как трусливый щенок бродишь по парочке досупных локаций и выхватываешь от всех подряд. Так становится скучно.....



It's quite sad that there's no "neither yes nor no" button.

It feels like there's no balance at all. To progress through the mission, you need skills that are located in other areas, but obtaining them is practically impossible. They load you up with tons of damage while offering extremely limited opportunities to upgrade weapons or armor. Some resources can only be obtained from specific monsters, but to reach them, you already need to be decently leveled up.

As a result, it's a vicious cycle—you wander around, constantly getting beaten up, without putting up much of a fight. On the one hand, it's intriguing to explore the world and experience the tension of taking down a boss, but for now, you're like a scared puppy roaming the few accessible locations and getting beaten by everyone you encounter. Eventually, it just gets boring...

和一代相比，2代的色彩更加丰富，武器道具和怪物种类也更多，虽然取消了二段跳和空中冲刺，但新加入的抓钩和浮空斗篷其实是令跳跳乐难度降低了的，同时可以引诱不同阵营的敌人互殴的设计也很出彩，且作为本作核心玩法的猎杀法师也十分有趣，突出一个量大管饱，但缺点也十分明显。缺少地图指引，堆怪严重，陷阱一坨，还有蹬墙跳的判定依旧狗屎以及为了让地图有捷径从而设计许多用不到的捷径这些一代已经存在的问题，在二代还是没有得到改善，同时最核心的法师追逐战设计的过于冗长，在缺少地图的情况下，每次都需要在清理过的地图小怪全部刷新的情况下追击6-7次以上才能亮血条进BOSS战，在这期间还要面对BOSS一直持续召唤的伤害离谱、攻击欲望极高的小怪围殴，这些都是折磨人的点，但随着流程的推进和等级的提升，中期伤害提上来后就舒服很多了，也慢慢的能找到推图探索收集的乐趣所在，这点其实有点像魂2。总之目前更新优化后的游戏还是蛮有趣的，可以一玩，但需要前期多一点耐心。

游戏有些地方着实是让人摸不到头脑，不理解为什么这么设计。先说药水需要采集素材，虽说只要看到就捡就不会缺，但这就纯属脱裤子放屁，无非就是增加工作量，根本就没有意义。然后这地图设计我个人认为十分的差，你跟一代一样有许多需要后期道具才能抵达的区域这没问题。增加一堆击杀一定数量法师才能开启的门算是怎么回事？就这种设计放到有地图的银河恶魔城也就算了，在你这游戏里有时候根本不记得在哪了好吗？地图设计不好还体现在一个方面，就是让人没有空间感。虽然一张地图我知道是一个整体，但我到处跑图脑海就勾勒不出来整个构造，无时无刻不在想“这TM是哪？”拜这点所赐，捷径完全没有发挥出“原来这是通往这里”的那种惊艳效果。最后再说说核心玩法，法师狩猎。我只能说抛去这部分，还挺像一代的。包括这部分，完全就是个缝合怪。有时候几个法师聚在一起，真是整个晋西北乱成了一锅粥，我都不知道我在打谁，谁在打我。有时候你得顶着各种机关地形去追法师，一个不小心就挂了。总之法师狩猎这部分体验是真的不尽如人意，你开门需要打法师，你追法师的时候很多地方就是囫囵吞枣探索了，这部分让游戏体验变得更糟。
然而还是得感慨一下，不愧是续作，给人的体验都是一模一样的。那就是，游戏有很多缺点，但如果你能接受这些缺点，还是能体验到乐趣的。我是2024年底玩的，这时已经没有刚发售时被怪物像鬼泣那样连招暴打的现象，整个体验下来还是不错的。现在的史低价格，我是觉得值得这个价。

The positive and negative reviews are both completely valid.  The game and its predecessor really shine in couch co-op metroidvania, and there's not tons of competition there, though this sequel seems to get some of the less fun parts of the original magnified.  My gripes, since I have to mark this as positive-- in my house we're hooked on both games and will queue up to get a third installment:
- The crafting and resource system is actually insane.  You always need more of resources that are impossible to get and there's no info in game about how you might go about getting them.  All of the crafting and upgrading UIs are filled with junk and difficult to navigate-- for example you set course early in the game to build specific types of weapons and you have to constantly sift through the blacksmith to find one of two or three viable weapons for your skill tree or level out of hundreds of weapons and armor that don't match your skills or are under- or over-leveled for you.  Absolutely frustrating.
- The game is filled with efforts to expand into multiplayer and factions and such that the original did not have.  As other reviews note, none of this makes sense to the player or is explained well in the game, and honestly it's just an extra layer of confusing junk on top of a game that is already willfully weird and confusing at its core.
- The game is insanely grind-y, and the sequel has a mechanic that basically has you running around the same maps to kill the same Mage 'bosses' again and again to get salt (XP) to make a minor upgrade in your skill tree since the bosses will definitely not drop materials you need to upgrade your weapons-- the grind of this game is essentially repetitive boss rush mode compared to the original, which had more exploring the map to grind a level.
- The end of the game has you make a final choice you'll want to look up before selecting-- at least one choice will overwrite your save to a New Game + where you'll no longer have the traversal skills or runes needed to keep exploring the other late game activities.  I have no idea why they don't just duplicate the save slots to have one before the final boss fight and one ready for NG+, but it's the reason I'll be trying to get my family into playing something else rather than playing this on and on and on.

This game gets a bad rap and I think it's unfair. Sure, it's not perfect and in some ways Salt and Sanctuary is better, but Sacrifice is really good too. 

Yes, the large interconnected world is gone, but it's replaced with huge interconnected levels connected to a hub a la Demon's Souls. Personally, I found these levels to be really good. I also like that they devs tried something rather than a straight 2D souls clone and introduced Monster Hunter style hunts. They can be tedious at times but I think they're really fun. I did play this game on launch on the PS5 and it did have one huge flaw: enemies could juggle you until you die. This was NOT fun at all. However, in the current version that is completely removed.

I definitely recommend Sacrifice if you liked Demon's Soul's or are keen for a Souls like with some MH influence.

算是玩了几天了，游玩时间也够十个退款时间了，最早的评测修修改改，现在下定决心写最后一个且不会再改的评测最终版。
整体思路与前作有非常大的不同，所以前作玩家初玩本作会很容易一脸懵逼+气急败坏。本作思路，每个新地图都是，开图，打第一个boss，打第一个法师，开门跑图，打第二个法师，再开门跑图，打第三个法师，然后就差不多解锁下一个地图了。装备不再靠捡和买，而是杀法师掉材料自己造，然后再用对应属性的强化材料强化。加入了没啥用的格挡和真正且挺好使用的完美格挡，不过boss和小兵都给了很多很多的零帧起手的攻击以及很高的韧性（滑稽）。
为了延长游戏时间啊，说实话前作我只用了现在一半的时间就全boss了，现在我这成就才三十来个。为了延长游戏时间，材料有爆率，你需要再刷什么无名法师啊，编号法师啊之类的，或者是自己找随机但频率老高且非常恶心人的野外法师。然后面对对方正义的围殴和使用你自己熟能生巧的跑图，以及只要没躺地上就没有无敌时间所以双手直接脱离键盘等复活，和削了坠落伤害但是到处都能坠崖且小兵（非精英怪）都送你起飞，等恶心的情况下击杀一个掉点材料但是没啥用的法师。强化材料除了最最最最最最低级的那些，其他的都要在地图的某些角落捡，或者是杀小兵几率掉，没错，地图小兵掉落的强化材料比法师掉的高一两级。还有，除了不刷新的怪会掉点好东西，其余大怪都基本不掉东西。
即使你不把一个图的冠名法师都杀完（通常有五个，但是杀三四个就够开图到下一个地图了），但是这不等于你见不到那些陌生人，甚至你在追当前地图第一个法师的时候，能碰到第二个法师来挡路。而一旦你杀了一个冠名法师，你就捅了法师窝了，前面地图的法师也会随机刷新来挡路，在沼泽地图开始会出现这情况。然后，你要面对越来越恶心的粪怪，地形，和打了又浪费时间，不打又总是打断你拉钩，左右横跳爬墙等操作。而且前面的法师到后面图伤害也会增加，被两个小兵两下打死是常有的事，尤其是爬墙爬阶梯的时候。
总的来说这游戏还是不错的，可能是作者上作太保守了，不尽心，这作想把自己la粪的水平表现出来，并体现出作者怀恋总是被路边的狗追着咬的思乡之情。
傻逼游戏，敌人是疯狗的，前后摇是没有的，投技是接连招的，追法师是跑断腿的，玩家性能是残疾的，韧性是虚设的，硬直是比命长的。
挺好玩的 先跑跑图开开捷径 追杀法师杀得他们屁滚尿流 法师间是敌对的 有时候别急着冲

Honestly surprised me to see how many people disliked this game, because I absolutely adore it. Definitely don't come in expecting a direct sequel to salt and sanctuary, but if you give this game a chance its lovely. tons of exploration and a surprising amount of depth for making builds, there definitely is a lot of silly stuff in terms of balance, but it kinda feels like elden ring to me where the game is pretty much as hard as you want it to be. Give it a shot especially if you can pick it up on sale.

Мне понравилась первая часть. Я прошёл её ещё на релизе и очень воодушевился, увидев, что выходит вторая.
Но увидев оценки и обзоры, я решил пока обождать. И вот сейчас, по скидке взял сразу обе и решил сперва перепройти первую. Прошёл, вспомнил старые времена и взялся за вторую часть.
Концепция второй части очень сильно отличается от первой. Первая типичная метроидвания, открываешь локи, убиваешь боссов, находишь "апгрейды", позволяющие тебе попасть в ранее закрыты участки и так до конца.
А во второй тебе надо убивать боссов-магов, которые очень похожи друг на друга. Огромный гуманоид с похожим мувсетом. И ладно бы просто убить, эти твари бегают от тебя по всей локации, пока ты их не загонишь на их арену, где проходит наискучнейший бой и так раз за разом.
Боже, я наиграл 6 часов и я УЖЕ НЕ ХОЧУ ВОЗВРАЩАТЬСЯ в неё. Это настолько скучно, нудно и однообразно. Это шагов 10 назад по сравнению с первой частью, где была огромная карта, классные локации, атмосфера и дух исследования.
Здесь же, ничего этого нет. Просто ходишь по 100 раз по одним и тем же местам, потом открываешь новые участки локации, ходишь там ещё дофига раз и так по кругу.
В первой части чувствовался прогресс, а здесь топтание на одном месте.

Перед тем как начал играть, я думал, что игроки просто слишком строгие к игре, что зря занижают оценки, я понимал, что вторая явно будет отличаться от первой, думал, что такие оценки, потому, что она ДРУГАЯ, а не ПЛОХАЯ.
Нет. Я ошибался. Игра ПЛОХАЯ, даже в отрыве от первой части.
Если и брать, то только по скидке, а лучше перепройти первую часть, что я скорее всего, и пойду делать, пройду ещё раз на НГ+.

魂和怪物猎人的杂交产物融入了一点只狼元素、仁王元素、恶魔之魂元素，加到后面估计自己都不知道在做什么了。整个游戏充斥着扭曲和混乱，这么多满分答卷摆在面前连抄都抄不明白
摒弃了前作的所有优点：
箱庭地图、宏大世界观、诡异阴沉的氛围、飙血量超大的爆发式画面张力和诺斯替教的神话表现风格
你可以吃到的史：
逆天的射击瞄准系统，瞄准角度和实际角度相差十万八千里，开弓先原地硬控自己两秒
怪物的性能人均但丁，普遍拥有零帧的抬手、疯狂的攻击欲望、顺畅的技能衔接以及极其强劲的韧性和冲击力，随手一个突刺就是三段伤害+击飞四个身位的高度、逃无可逃的追击能力，妄想脱战喝果粒橙的话下一秒就一个大跳跨越整个屏幕无视地形飞到脸上还附带一个平A，并且这个大跳 [b] 没有冷却时间 [/b]

技能树从前代精美的符文式摆放变成了杂乱的一堆跟缠成一团的头发丝一样，描述背景的碎片化叙事变成了百来个无名小卒的牢骚日记，恨不得直接把敷衍两个字写在脸上
狭小的横版空间再加上变态的BOSS技能和小怪组合，就像把玩家关在小黑屋里用各种刑具施虐
[b] 我不会推荐任何人购买这个游戏 [/b]

Salt and Sacrifice is an RPG with its predecessor style - Salt and Sanctuary -  that didn't follow the Dark Soul formula and instead went through a Monster Hunter approach. 

Compared to Salt and Sanctuary, it lets players have a more relaxed time fighting new bosses by farming the equipment from the previous ones that appear as mages, hence Monster Hunter vibes rather than Dark Soul - you lose your gathered ''salt'' for leveling if you don't use it before getting killed - you can cheese ''former bosses / mages'' by kiting them one against the other (at least in the first zone).

It has huge improvements on cooperative gameplay as you can even play from the same PC/station, plugging two gamepads for example (PVE, PVP, split screen, couch coop, etc... are all on the table).

The ''story'' doesn't look to me as appealing as SKA Studio's first game SaS, as it seems it unfolds almost all in the beginning - Salt and Sanctuary's was better narrated perhaps (?). 

Combat system is similar - if not same - as its first one, but you get upgrades much faster - or at least that is my impression, not something that I dislike as I go for an easier gameplay, but it strides further away from its predecessor - and I guess this might be one of the main reasons why this game has mixed reviews on Steam at the moment.

All in all I like it and Salt and Sacrifice doesn't have anything less than other Metroidvanias/RPG/etc... on the market.

前作と比べて評価が低いが自分は前作も今作もなかなか面白いと思うダクソ系２DアクションRPG

ダークソウル系の死にげーが２DアクションRPGになったみたいなの聞いて面白そうと思ったなら前作も今作も含めて楽しめると思う、ただ前作、今作もスキルツリーでの初期化が一つずつしかできないうえに数が限られているので一旦全部初期化などはできません、注意しましょう、前作と比べて今作の評価が低い理由の一つ(大体これのせい)に一度クエストを受けてボス(魔導士)を出現させると倒しても倒しても勝手にフィールドに出現するようになります、探索すればするほどマップに魔導士が３～５体とか出てきて鬱陶しい、しかもダメージを与えると逃げて別のマップに移動しますそれを繰り返し追い詰めて倒さないといけないので面倒などが主な理由です、ボスと取り巻きはそのボスの装備素材を落とすのでそれを作るのに倒す価値はあるのですが、２～３回倒すと大体一式作れるくらい素材落としてくれるのでその辺のバランスも含めてやっぱり探索の邪魔なだとなってしまうようです、自分はその辺はあんまり苦でもなかったので前作同様今作も普通に楽しめました。

ソロで３週くらいクリアしたくらい好きです、今は最近フレンドが買ったので二人で最初からやっていますが、マルチだと相方がタゲ取ってくれてる間に後ろから魔法とか飛び道具連発すればすぐ倒せるのでマルチでの難易度はめちゃくちゃ低いです、とりあえず前作も今作もセールだとかなり安くなるのでバンドルで前作と今作一緒に興味あれば買ってもいいと思います。
Tim Burton's Metroidhuntersouls

Do (tentatively) recommend! Only if it's on sale, though. The current gameplay experience isn't bad, but given its... quirks...  I don't think it's worth full price. 

- Maps aren't interconnected anymore, it's all accessed via a main hub a la Demon's Souls. That being said, I don't like how every time you go back to the hub, you're placed so far away from the main portal. You also cannot warp between areas without returning to the main hub.

- There is no Bell of Return equivalent; if you need to get back to the hub quickly, your best bet is to just exit to main menu and reload. Otherwise, have fun running around the map!

- Enemies are [i]obscenely[/i] aggressive, and any enemy that blocks does [i]not take chip damage[/i]. Even knights in the first area that only block with a sword don't take chip damage from a [i]vertical bolt of lightning[/i]. The only "chip damage" that I've seen that makes it through is status damage, like from the ice stave's icicle missiles. And that sucks, because the player [i]does[/i] take chip damage, in addition to status damage from enemies while blocking. That disparity isn't good.

- Knockback is crazy. If you thought being constantly command-grabbed by Spindlebeasts in Sanctuary was bad, wait until you see two or three enemies (or a mage) posted up on a ledge that you have to get up onto in order to progress, and each of them are likely to chain attack you with staggered animations so the only thing you can do is roll, or knock you right back off the ledge, often killing you. May the gods have mercy upon you if you have no ranged options.

- Mages, the main big bad type in the game, roam the map. Which is fine. What's not fine is their endlessly summoned fodder having crazy amounts of health, damage, and knockback, particularly when multiple mages can inhabit the same space. There is friendly fire, which helps, but it's still an absolute fustercluck.

- The NPC quests are actually pretty nice! I love the idea of following an NPC around the different maps and it culminating in a [spoiler]unique boss fight[/spoiler] that you wouldn't have gotten otherwise. I do wish you actually got more than just armor from the NPCs that [spoiler]turn into bosses[/spoiler]. It also feels like armor and weapons from enemy drops aren't nearly as varied as they were in Sanctuary? Nearly everything of substance is crafted from mages, which tracks, because they're the big bad and it feeds the Monster Hunter style gameplay loop, but given how exploration heavy the game is, I'd have loved more non-mage weapon and armor options.

- Weapons have built-in, unchangeable skills/spells. Gone are the days of having incantations on your toolbelt like in Sanctuary. Your only method of mix-and-matching is by equipping two different weapons, and that doesn't go too far. In addition, you need to invest points into skills to use the weapon type [i]and[/i] the skill type that the weapon uses (either Forbidden Glyphs or Divine Glyphs). So you have to grind and invest in an absolute minimum of three different skill trees up to Class 5 in order to be the most effective with your chosen weapon type and your chosen armor type. Woe betide you if you decide you want to use a specific weapon's divine glyphs if you didn't spec for it.

- Speaking of weapons, the disparity in weapon scaling is so... awful. There are absolute tons of weapons with E, D, or C in scaling and extremely few with S in most weapon types. Some weapon info for you, from the wiki.
1) Staves - 13 weapons, none of them have S scaling.
2) Bludgeons - 12 weapons, and SIX of them have S scaling in strength
3) Glaives - 9 weapons; one of them has S scaling in dexterity, and another has S scaling in both dexterity and strength.
4) Greatblade - 13 weapons, and only one of them has S scaling at all, in both strength and conviction.
5) Greathammer - 10 weapons; none of them have S scaling, and only one has A scaling.
6) Halfspear - 13 weapons and only one of them has an A scaling in dexterity, no S scaling in sight.
7) Highblades (katanas) - 9 weapons, and only one of them has A scaling in dexterity. And that one has no special abilities.
8) Rapiers - 9 weapons, and not one of them even has A in dexterity scaling.
9) Scythes - 9 weapons, and all of them have worse than B scaling.
10) Twindagger - 9; 3 of them have S scaling, and two of those have no runic art abilities. The third's runic art just increases attack damage.
11)  Two-hander - 13 weapons and none of them have A scaling. The highest is B on a weapon that has no runic arts.
12) Whips - 9 weapons, there's FIVE with S scaling.
13) Ranged weapons (which vary in form but they all go in the same slot) - roughly 36 weapons, only two have S scaling in a stat, one in strength and one in luck.

This game absolutely NEEDS an intensive, lovingly crafted balance patch, like the one Sanctuary got. I like the game! It's fun, unique enough, and the lore is interesting with a couple of nice twists... But the final boss (and many others) should not be able to be stunlocked to death with one of the first weapons you get in the game. Early game weapons shouldn't be nearly that powerful compared to later game weapons. 
But on the other side of that coin, the bosses shouldn't [i]need[/i] to be stunlocked for the player to feel like they have a chance, given some of the questionable hitboxes and obscenely aggressive enemy behavior that allows for some frustrating experiences.

Is the game good? Yeah, I think so. But it doesn't feel done. It needs rebalancing to a high degree.
10/10 Cannot get enough

Didn't realize I wanted to yap about this game that much but here we are, so as a brief aside before my tl;dr'd review, I played Salt and Sanctuary to death until they let that modder make updates and removed all the speedrun tech they said they'd leave in on lobos jr's stream way back when (very cool James), and played through this game a lot more on epic back on launch, but finally got it here so I figured I'd make a review. Also Just want to say if somehow someway the devs do see this, or if someone shows them, or whatever else, hi James and Michelle, even after all my years playing your games and my issues with em I still value them clearly or I wouldn't be writing this so like huge thanks for shaping a part of my thoughts on all games moving forward pretty heavily all things considered, and don't nerf any of the weapons I point out when I talk about the atrocious game balance cause it's bad but it's really funny 😁😁 god writing that made me feel so parasocial.

Alright pros and cons, do keep in mind that I do not think this is a good game I just think the issues are so big that I stopped letting them ruin my fun.

- The bosses (not mages) all suck really bad, have no real drops, and basically just read as rough drafts for what should be there when the game got out of beta. This rings especially true for the final boss, who has basically no build up, absolutely negative aura, and I don't think after all my playthroughs I can even remember what his attacks are or what he even looks like, genuinely just some random in my game, and holds up even worse when compared to the final boss of Salt and Sanctuary.
The bosses in the Heart of Altarstone (semi dlc area) are slightly better, barring the one that is literally just a reskin duo boss of 2 regular bosses LOL, but the rest are at least really memorable to me, the four divines don't even feel like they got tested and are in the wrong game and the pot dude was just really funny, shockwave hitting behind him and near unreactable last phase dash followup aside, and Barix is probably my favorite enemy in the whole game barring that reskinned Ronin Cran leap that felt way too fast sometimes, and the closest the game gets to a boss having a real boss drop, even if it's given to you by an npc after the fight. Just wish he had more lore and a memorable OST, otherwise he actually holds up pretty well to the Nameless King and kind of gives me Artorias vibes (ie coolest boss being an optional dude with sword).

- The mages themselves are all neat concepts I'd rather read in a book, and chasing them always feels super unrewarding. 
I still don't actually know if damaging them before they get to the end ever matters. Like if I do 50K damage or just 1000 it doesn't ever seem to matter until it's the boss fight part I just wish you could kill them earlier. Also the summons taking super reduced damage when they spawn in is super annoying since they always get an attack off before it's gone. For the bulk of the game being about them I can't say it's a fun or very replayable concept. Especially when

- The balancing SUCKS. Ok so in the first game it wasn't much better, the Scharfrichter was just op in every way and magic was way too overtuned, and prayers were a must have for NG+, but in this game? You have chainsaw m2s that do like 8 kajillion dps, the rothook one shotting training dummies (tier 3 btw), actually weirdly enough for just straight up no buffs ar the top 6 weapons have like 4 glaives in there although most of them actually just suck cause no glyphs. Ranged weapons are also just divided by is it a thrown item (bad), channeling rod (bad), bow (bad), crossbow (good/best if using a specific one no spoilers 😁), throwing dagger, or throwing axe (best, fleshhunter having the highest ar if you use the cold charm, otherwise the carrots do)(also what is up with the flesh mage just having the best weapons in game bro is actually insane).

A large portion of your stats also come down to artifacts which are randomly generated to increase different stats like a gacha game (they're even called artifacts I'd actually be a little surprised if it wasn't inspired), like, genuinely why does this exist I have no clue but your best bet is legit just reloading a save when bolstering them if you get bad rolls (you will).

Also idk what the devs were smoking when they designed the armor but I want some cause like why are the best heavy armors in game like tier 4s and 2s barring the hallowed set I assume the tier 5s are just tier 5 cause their weight to stat ratio is higher but like I'm too lazy to check and who cares about that when if you're using heavy armor you probably just want resistances and don't care about slow rolling anyway. Plus who even needs to roll when

- The parry system is hilarious. In Sanctuary? You can only parry mobs (bosses just make the sparks and hit you anyway) and if you do you just get a ripost if possible plain and simple. In this game? You can parry literally anything except maybe grabs I didn't test that and parrying is more like sekiro requiring a few parries to get a riposte and I think the same for bosses but I never figured out how to do that reliably cause sometimes they just do not get staggered after like 20.

- Also quite frankly, the amount of reused sounds from Sanctuary is kind of insane, like so much of my gameplay is just broken up by "oh hey I recognize that one" that it get's a bit comical.

- And as far as the ost goes no hate making music is hard I get it but man there just is not enough songs in this game it's actually kind of sad how little music there is. 

- Also for how many NPCS there are you might imagine a bit more lore but somehow I feel like there's actually less of a coherent story or lore than the first game and that was almost all just dark souls item descriptions or worse, broken up stories in the skill tree. Those exist here too but this game is so undocumented that there isn't a readily available lore doc with them connected together so I ain't reading allat. 

- Additionally, the area's all feel pretty different which isn't saying much when there's only like 5 of them but man would it kill the devs to remove like just a few of the enemies just directly on a ledge that can block projectiles or similarly irritating enemy placements. Like I get in dark souls occasionally you'll get hit by a hollow hiding behind a wall and be like "oh you silly game you sure got me!" but the keyword there is occasional. It did not feel occasional in this game dawg it's like turn a corner WHAM! walk back into comically placed rake into 8 of those electric ♥♥♥♥♥ in the Heart of Altarstone, or 8 of those dagger ♥♥♥♥♥ in the Heart of Altarstone, or 8 of those you get the point the enemy placement is really bad.

- One last thing, the game says it includes online co-op stuff, like helping you fellow inquisitors or hunting them or whatever, but this game is dead. You will not find engaging co-op pve or pvp experiences here. There just aren't enough players for that. Luckily you can get all the online tokens offline or the best weapon would be gated behind an inaccessible requirement but otherwise yea no it's just not gonna happen, and for the best if I'm honest. The online experience on launch was absolutely abysmal, imagine if you were fighting a mage in Elden Ring but it was in 2d so you just have to lock the ♥♥♥♥ in and parry every single hit or you just die on the spot.

Overall I'd recommend this game as an experience, a lesson on do's and don'ts, that sort of thing. I still wish they didn't sell out to Epic on launch so maybe the game could've gotten real updates on launch and might not have died.

Oh well, no point in dreaming.

Некоторые монстры частично или полностью перекочевали из нее. Имеется столько же разветвленное древо прокачки и мрачные запутанные локации.
Но есть и существенные отличия.
Здесь нету слота для щита, но есть тип оружия авангард которое содержит щит и меч или щит и молот/топор, которое позволяет полностью блокировать урон от атак.
Большинство видов оружий делаются из боссов-магов, и имеют один или даже два стихийных урона, а так же набор заклинаний которые использовал маг.
Имеется развитая система магии. Как уже сказано выше, заклинания привязаны к оружию, некоторые из них требуют концентрацию, которую можно восстановить зельями, другие ярость, которая копится в бою.
В игре имеется и элемент кастельвании - постепенно вы будете находить предметы, которые позволят вам забираться в места, куда ранее вы не могли попасть. А крюк-кошку можно и эффектно использовать в бою.

В игре более двух десятков магов, из каждого можно сделать набор брони и аксессуаров по 4-6 оружий с 1-4 заклинаниями. Так что можно долго экспериментировать и пробовать разные варианты экипировки, которые наиболее подойдут вашему стилю игры.

Так же здесь кооператив, и пвп, как и в дарксоулс. Можно как играть в кооперативе, так и присоединяться и и помогать проходить боссов случайным игрокам, или самому искать помощи. Ну а так же вторгаться и пытаться убить других игроков. В игре есть 6 "ковенантов" со своими особенностями и целями, а так же наборами оружия и брони, некоторые из оружий имеют уникальные механики.

Так же есть новая игра +, так что при желании можно прокачать героя так, что он сможет эффективно использовать любое оружие.

В целом, я в восторге от этой игры, и не представляю почему отзывы у нее неоднозначные, возможно на выходе она была сыровата, но сейчас все отлично.

When I first started the game, I was not really enjoying my time. It seemed like a dumbed down version of the first game. Then I began enjoying it a little more when the level exploration kicked in. But after a couple of hours, I stumbled into the mage fights, which are really the dumbest part of this game.

I cannot comprehend how somebody had the idea to put those mage fights into the game. Chasing the same 4-5 types of mages, 2 times per level, for every level. The worst part is how they sometimes get gobbled together, spamming ennemies. Mages already beaten even sometimes spawn right on top of your save point or block your path when trying to get to the mage you're trying to kill.

The game would be fun otherwise, but to progress, you have to kill mages. And they will then respawn anywhere on the level and kill your fun. And since the mages count as bosses, there's really not that much variety in boss design or number per level. The mages suck up everything that would be good in the game and they replace it with repeatitive and boring gameplay.

过多的跳跳乐一类的地图设计，有些跳跳乐设计真是一坨，有怪先不能说，整个流程地图有3/4全是极限跳，还有一些阴间的跳跳乐设计，比如三块会碎的板中间放一个旋转，被打到掉底就直接死了，不过还不行，这种设计出来，是你作者想看玩家死了又死跑图到这里再死重新跑吗？有些真的多余了，适量适量，太多玩的时候越来越烦躁，游戏本身正反馈就不行，就这一点点的正反馈全部花在了跑图和跳跳乐上，熬到boss，boss还没跑图难，有些地图阴间是真阴间。
还有那个逼篝火，你们他妈会做吗，就应要做个假篝火，跟你妈脑残一样，分真假篝火的意义在哪里？假篝火跟真篝火区别就是差了一个传送，假篝火能传真篝火，真篝火不能传假篝火，你他妈一张图最多就三个篝火，而且间隔非常远，，两个真篝火间差不多要两个假的串连，两个假的覆盖地图面积还是非常大的，做的对的是在boss门口放了一个篝火，但是，他妈非得放一个假的，就会导致玩家打完boss，会有很多盐和银，带着往前推进风险是很多玩家不愿意接受的，会偏向于先回家花完，这时候你用假篝火传回家，再会到打完boss这里就需要先从家里走到家里最下面，再选传送点，传到地图起始点，然后再选里目的地最近的真传送点，最后跑一大段图到目的地，没有近的真篝火，恭喜你，你得重新跑图，怪还在，阴间跳跳乐也在。
boss也是一个非常的问题，你如果boss做的好，玩家跑半天图，最后见到boss，能感觉这个boss做的不错，打起来很有意思，难一点都没关系，甚至还会包容你们做的这个傻逼关卡设计，boss就两个我觉得还行的，就一个绿猎人和留守二人，绿猎人作为游戏前期boss做的还可以，就好比魂三的古达，一板一眼，半血后招式有一些变化却不是很大，作为新人老师做的很可以，留守二人，做为类魂或者类银河城这类游戏里面最难做的二人组boss，做的也刚刚好，不会像两只疯狗一样，一直输出，或者一个输出完另一个马上补上，盐二这个两个boss会给玩家留时间，给玩家在两个boss的间隙里抓机会，会有一种操作的的感觉，那你们不是会做boss的吗？你们其他boss做的是你吗的什么东西，我操死你的吗，越做越敷衍，一点心意没，还有那个原子法师，你用你妈的胩裆做的？什么强度，不仅能压起身，出手还快，重型武器根本出不了手，做的时候有没有动过脑子？自己有没有玩过自己的游戏？跑图给玩家烦躁的感觉，打完boss也没什么成就感，你们自己不反思一下吗?甚至还有很多的怪和boss的外形和攻击是照搬1的。
武器也非常的不平衡，虽然1这个问题也没做的很好，还多加一个黄金酒，但是2更明显，重武器又短又慢，出手比不过怪是很常见的，削刃和冲击很差，轻武器伤害很低低削韧，小怪面对轻武器很多都是出招霸体，有些武器模组过分鸡肋，像鞭子，而且整体武器的战技鸡肋，格挡很强很阴，法师和咒师强，平衡很差，而且不职业相比，武器整体强度偏弱，不像1整体武器偏强，一些武器特别出色。两代都没做好。武器设计里还有一个最脑瘫的，强化素材乱设计，总共有5种还是6种强化素材，一种有五阶，一把武器会出现需要两种强化素材，其实这都还好，最傻逼的是强化素材获得很麻烦，这就不得不提到全做最傻逼的设计，狩猎法师，除了地图上捡（很少，种类也不一定是玩家需要的），要靠刷法师来获得，这个狩猎法师巨麻烦，要追着法师全图跑杀一个法师至少十分种左右，期间得不停跑图打怪，非常折磨，我有这时间，不如导一管。不仅武器要护甲也要强化素材，需求大，巨折磨。
联机初次尝试可以理解，但是做的太刻意了，这个2d联机帮帮还有点意义，这个入侵做的有啥意思，入侵别人你个2d游戏能怎么玩？能怎么体现操作，往往就是上来对a，有什么意思？你有不像法环和魂，需要操作，你这个能干啥？游戏过程又给了很多联机道具，还有很多武器是需要入侵成功换的，你他妈有日活吗，你连你的吗！
把这个狩猎法师留到最后，实在是忍不了，整个游戏最最最傻逼的设定，像你妈作者用他妈屁眼上的痔疮想出来的，纯纯的傻逼。因为这个脑残设计一体地图没有，变成了章节，你要说作者不会那是不可能的，盐1的地图设计是我看过2d里做的最好的，比空洞骑士还好，甚至没有小地图我都是可以接受的，盐2因为这个狩猎法师直接抛弃了这种出色的地图设计，就为了防止法师满地图乱跑和bug，几张地图互相屁关系没有，完全的割裂，盐2还删除了隐藏门，多半也是因为这个。几个法师重复度极高，每张图都能看见，只是换个名字换个数值，你还得继续追他们，本来不是硬性的就可以不管，哎，不行，作者认为这个设计是他毕生最有脑的设计了，你们必须玩，不仅上面提的强化素材需要，你不杀到一定数量，地图一些区域你是去不了的，必须打，就像玩家知道这个狩猎法师是一坨屎，作者还要硬给你喂下去，你说这个作者是不是司马了。劳资这么期待2，你他妈做这个给我？
玩下来，看到了只看到了制作组的野心和想法，其他全是大粪，臭不可闻，你但凡跟1玩法一样，剧情是1的后续解读或者不是我都可以买单。你给我玩这个你真是死全家了，你就非得惦记你那个逼狩猎法师，老子狩猎你的马！
一年时间了，什么都没改，看着这可怜的日活和评测，也无动于衷，你们制作组有点诚意吗？制作组全家死光光

总体上来说：不推荐这款游戏。
如果你是前作《盐与避难所》已经达成白金的玩家的话，会发现这部许多地方的改动十分别扭，难受，举例来说：

1.小怪的强韧度十分不合理，只要防御，就是无敌姿态，不会被破盾、削韧，大部分时候很难处理大面积敌人以及灵活敌人。

2.反观玩家的性能十分差劲，本作中玩家的强韧度很低，经常容易被击飞，且没有无敌时间，因此经常在被击飞后收到连击导致死亡，也容易在跳跃时被弓箭击飞摔落。

3.最不满意的一点就是战斗系统和跑图系统。战斗方面，大改了防御和弹反机制，防御没有对应的属性防御了，这让面对属性攻击时十分难受；其次在弹反上，本作的弹反改为了类似只狼的精准防御后削韧处决，熟悉了前作弹反的玩家，很难适应这一改变，原因在于：前作中弹反处决是越级击杀高级别敌人、简便击杀疯狗敌人的最好方式，本作中直接削没了，让游戏难度大幅度提升。
  另外，游戏中取消了双手搭配的功能，改成了类似于怪猎的武器系统，这样的改动让游戏的武器搭配变得索然无味，只要不断地更新、升级装备就行，不需要考虑防御和武器搭配了。同时，也少了很多花式玩法
  跑图系统中，玩家像瘸子，有疾跑功能却不能跑跳、没有冲刺和空中冲刺，取而代之的是少数时候可以使用的钩爪和滑索，本身玩家在空中的机动性就不足，缺失了空中机动性，更容易在击飞后被连击致死

I tried to like this game.  I even made 2 characters, one a heavy weapon wielding, heavy armor user, and one a fast sword, light armor user.  Neither are fun enough to overcome the massive flaws in this game's design.  The core of the game seems to be based around hunting mages who are terrorizing the places or whatever ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ lore these games cobble together to try and tie together the gameplay.  But anyway, the main gameplay loop is following these mages around wherever the ♥♥♥♥ they teleport off to every 5 seconds, catching up to them, dealing with the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ enemies they spam-spawn at you, while the other basic enemies in the level attack you, and repeat for way longer than any amount of fun would last before getting into a half-assed boss fight with the mage in which every single attack sends you flying like you're in a pinball machine.  The fights aren't overly difficult, but they are incredibly annoying.  It is so tedious having to follow around the mage forever, repeatedly, and deal with enemy spam the whole way, only to be rewarded with a simplistic boss fight whose only difficulty comes from the strain it puts on your sanity from sheer tedium.  Attack, roll through the boss, attack, roll through the boss, repeat, again and again and again until you wanna ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ vomit.  And it's not just the mages, it's EVERY boss. While I personally prefer the art and world of Sanctuary more, Sacrifice is a great follow-up. Where the first game feels akin to the first Dark Souls, this feels closer to Dark Souls 3 as it's structured around a hub from which you teleport to levels. It also implements mage hunts, which are very similar to the gameplay loop in a Monster Hunter title. While it can be fun, more often than not I feel it clashes with the area exploration and main bosses. Overall, the combat itself feels smooth, the co-op is improved, faction quest centered on multiplayer invasions aren't fun. I recommend.

Boy, oh, boy.

Disclaimer: I'm writing as I currently try to look deep down into my soul to find any will to finish the game.

First of all, as far as I've gone into the game, it's not as bad as reviews may make it look like. Is it good tho, you ask? Eh...no.

This game would maybe be a hit if it wasn't part of the Salt IP. Many (myself included) loved Salt and Sanctuary for its approach on the Souls formula which had a nice and unique combination of souls and metroidvania elements. Sadly, tho, Sacrifice tried to combine Souls and Monster Hunter into a single game and I really don't think it worked as intended. 

My main complain so far is that 1. the hub succs and 2. the game is easy (so far, at least). 

1. Hub just too big and with many things in it. I honestly believe I spend more time getting lost in the hub and walking from one side of it to another as much as I have actually played the game.

2. I'm no pro Salt player nor have gone far into the game but...the difficulty so far seems lacking and very artificial. Enemies are either spammers or extremely slow. I've done 4 bosses so far (2 mages included) not having dying even once to them. I don't even know what their patterns were, as they were too simple. 
This really saddens me, as I really liked the first 3 or 4 bosses from Salt and Sanctuary. Sunken Knight is a masterpiece of a boss, with a cool pattern and well balanced difficulty. Can't say I think that's the case for Sacrifice. 

There are tons of changes they've made to the game, compared to the Sanctuary counterpart. The game is not terrible, but I'd recommend it more to MH fans than for Souls fans (Sanctuary fans included).

So, basically, Salt and Sacrifice is the Dark Souls 2 of the Salt IP.

I really like this game, played it a lot when it released on the other store, but *big disclaimer* despite many similarities between it and Sanctuary, it plays extremely different, mostly in a macro way, but also in several important micro ways; focusing on incremental gear progression and building equipment sets around damage types. 

The atmosphere is extremely different, but still really good, and while world exploration is still a major aspect of gameplay, it's definitely taken a backseat in comparison to it's predecessor, with the focus primarily now being on hunting bosses down through levels, and backing them into an arena to finish them off for their body parts. After collecting enough parts, usually only taking between one and a small handful of battles depending on what exactly you're hoping to make, you can take them to your home base for quick and simple crafting of new armour, weapons, etc.

Big things I especially enjoy are the art direction, music/sound design; and the way you can feel your character build progress steadily but surely forward. These are also some of the major factors for my. and many others. love of it's predecessor, so the ways this game diverges tonally and otherwise is often very jarring for people, but I strongly feel that while different choices were made in how these aspects of the game are expressed, they are no less strong than what Salt and Sanctuary had to offer.

Jumping directly from one to the other is definitely quite jarring, changes to the core combat can create a feeling of loss, the two biggest culprits to me being the change from having an active parry in the predecessor, to now timing a perfect block in this game, and only having one slot for your weapon; meaning if you want a shield it's going to be packaged together with a specific weapon. Both of these changes feel like combat has been stripped down, but that complexity still exists in the several new equipment slots, and synergies now allowed by the new properties equipment can, and almost always will, have.

In summery, definitely not just "Salt and Sanctuary 2" but still very good in it's own way, with a definite shared essence.

很显然盐2不适用这个规则
这游戏大部分敌人的ai就是见面跑攻，远距离就接近，中距离二度跑攻，近距离无限精力巨剑七连斩，很少会有保持距离对峙的举动，远程敌人就是魂1王城双弓射个爽，被接近了就掏出巨匕首五连斩，可能在视野外就已经上好了各种猴戏buff所以近战伤害比远程还高

除了投技外，各种基础机制也非常怪，比如盐2取消了前作的弹反，改为完美防御削减对方架势，可是完美防御自己的硬直时间可能比敌人还长，并且处决敌人过程中自己不回绿，敌人被处决或使用完投技后还能抢在你恢复之前一顿超级大乱砍

D.跳跃躲避投技然后被啃，不敢翻滚起身于是被无限连死

建议的加点方向和制作武器
1图点1级大刀，用捡到的白板大刀砍BOSS，多余的点数加2级双巨和3级神圣字符
2图风法双巨，快速武器，重击和跑攻优秀，战技实用，最好第一次打风法的时候多拣点小怪掉落早点做出来，满足风法双巨的要求后开始加3级小锤和4级禁忌字符

Don't get misled by the art style, this is a very different experience from Sanctuary.

First of all, I'm a huge fan of the previous game, over the years it has become one of my comfort games where I can easily sit down and enjoy going through the game once a year.

Sacrifice is a much more focused, but at the same time un-focused, type of game. No interconnected levels (which could be a deal breaker for some) where you have a specific objective: Kill the Mages.

These levels are not that complicated to explore but, whenever you return to the main hub of the game and want to go back to a previously explored level, the checkpoints reset and you have to go through the whole level again. Since the exploration has been taken down by a notch I would have loved a handy map just to eliminate that sense of being lost every time you go back to some levels.

Very nice additions to the combat system and to the traversal but the biggest downgrade comes from the enemy design. 
This game was made to be played in Co-op, and it shows!

Enemies usually are very tanky, they are very unpredictable and they have some unfair and cheap attacks, like a very fast startup animation that goes instantly into an extremely fast grab which, of course, takes more than half of your health.

When deciding if you want to buy this game, keep that in mind, you might need a friend to enjoy it at its fullest.

This game is what would happen if Monster Hunter and Dark Souls had a baby, while making it grow up to become a Metroidvania.  If you are a fan of those three things, than you are going to love this game.  The combat revolves around attacking, rolling, blocking, stamina conservation, etc.  You can easily acquire the gear that you want by killing the Mage with said gear a few times.  

I played this game on launch on Playstation, and I have just recently gotten back into it by buying it on Steam.  I really do not have anything bad that I could say about this game.  

The world feels alive, meaning the Mages and enemies fight against each other.  There are many different types of Mages that you will encounter, and they all feel unique.  Each Mage will summon some enemies that you will need to kill in order to farm the drops needed to create a weapon, armor, or assessory from that mage.

Overall this game is an absolute gem, and I really hope some people give it a shot.

這代主打的就是獵殺法師，
而這遊戲所有的機制和地圖設計就是以此作為構築。
所以你要把自己當作一個獵人，把要攻略的法師看作獵物，
慢慢提升自己的狩獵技巧，裝備，與經驗，從而面對並挑戰每個狩獵目標。
儘管法師受了傷會逃跑，甚至有機率同時遭遇兩至三個法師，
但這遊戲真的很佛心，
首先，法師彼此都是相互對立的，並非同個陣營，這意味著當你看到兩隻法師，
你該高興而不是生氣，因為這代表另一隻法師會成為你的隊友幫你削減目標血量。
然後你需要做的僅僅只是等待，等你的狩獵目標血量被削減到一定程度，他自會逃跑，
然後你要做的就是去追尋你原本的目標，其他都不用管。
其次，是這遊戲佛心的第二處，
逃跑時的法師是會留下蹤跡的，所以你絕對不可能找不到他們，
而且他們會出現的地圖也是此刻的你一定能走到的地方，
所以不用怕他們不會消失不見。
第三，法師逃跑的路徑中，
經常伴有一到二的石碑，讓你回復血瓶和弓箭至全滿狀態，
所以你狩獵的過程中真的不需要擔心自己補給不足，走幾步回去就到了，相當方便。
最後，也是最重要的，
就是，法師不會回血，法師不會回血，法師不會回血，
在最後法師停下也就是BOSS戰的位置，後方必定有石碑，即便死掉也能馬上復活。
然後追趕過程被打掉的血量也不會恢復，所以要殺死他們真的不難，
難的反而是追逐的過程，但這過程其實熟了也不難，尤其當你狩獵技巧越來越高時。
很多抱怨這個機制的只是因為不習慣，就像當初人們不習慣魂類遊戲的機制，
但實際上當你熟悉了這套體系，你會覺得遊戲過程還挺有趣的。
甚至還會以這套系統為樂，
對我來說二代其實比一代更好玩，因為一開始的你真的很渺小，
可能隨便一個高階法師都能幾招秒掉你，
但當你成長的足夠強大時，立場是反過來的，變成是你追著他們跑，
那種快感和成就感是一代沒辦法體會到的。
所以比起一代我更喜歡二代，當然每個人看法都不同，
但這款遊戲絕對沒有頻論說的那麼不堪，
它是一款耐著性子玩下去絕對能體會箇中滋味的好遊戲。

Вторая часть экшнрпг с закосом под соулслайк неприятно удивила. Минусов в игре оказалось больше, чем плюсов. 
+ разнообразие пушек, не в последнюю очередь из-за способностей
+ в игре присутствуют интересные для исследования локации
+ куча косметики в виде снаряжения

На этом плюсы, к сожалению, закончились, далее то, что получилось спорно и плохо
- На каждой локации в определенных точках есть трупы магов, которые открывают опцию охоты на последнего. Некоторые места в локации открываются после определенного количества убитых таким образом магов. НО сама охота на магов это какой-то цирк. Беготня по локации и долбежка лкм/пкм в течении 10 минут, причем эти 10 минут маг просто спавнит пару болванов, дай бог выдаст одну атаку и убежит от вас. И повторяться это будет не раз и не два, а ВСЮ игру. При этом получается забавная ситуация - после убийства такого мага, он сможет рандомно заспавниться в локации. А теперь представьте, что таких магов 5 на локацию. Мало того что это превращает игру в клоунаду, так еще и напрочь ломает нарратив. Ведь после убийства мага общее число, что мы видим УВЕЛИЧИВАЕТСЯ и сколько с ними не бейся лучше не станет.
- в игре поломанная прогрессия из-за чего она становится СЛИШКОМ простой. В первое прохождение я убивал магов пачками - так как с них падают материалы на крафт оружия/брони и материалы для прокачки оружия (а еще, как было описано выше, они спавнятся сами). В то же время с них падает и местная экспа и деньги. По итогу уже к концу первой трети игры у меня было столько урона, что обычные враги лопались с удара, а боссы с десятка. И игра стала СЛИШКОМ простой. После этого я решил больше не качать силу (перед этим было вкачано 37). Все очки уходили в выносливость и волю (для стамины). По итогу в конце игры эти статы были примерно 40/40/26. Проблем с уроном все еще не было, как и (к моему удивлению) со здоровьем. Поитогу любой босс отлетал с максимум второй попытки и ощущался как еще один болван.
= Локации в общем интересные, но лучше всего запомнилась первая, так как туда нужно возвращаться еще не раз после обнаружения нового способа перемещения (метроидвания все же ага). Но в целом, локации довольно середняковые и все представляют из себя платформы разных форм, которые летают друг над другом. Из интересных могу вспомнить гробницу с ловушками из второй локации. А так, каких-то новых челленджей ни локации ни противники на протяжении всей игры не предоставляют. Стоит однако отметить локацию Traitors Tomb. Там и задумка интересная и противники необычные и единственный босс, который может заставить неподготовленного игрока пропотеть. Я, к примеру, пришел туда на полном расслабоне, тк думал, что вся игра уже будет проще некуда. Я ошибся. Рекомендую идти туда как можно раньше.
- зерг раш. Противников много. С прохождением по головам магов НЕПРИЛИЧНО много.

В целом несбалансированная неинтересная игра, которая не удивляет до последней минуты. Прошел исключительно из-за желания поиграть в соулслайк

[h1]Похоже, соль не идёт на пользу...[/h1]
Ох уж это продолжение Salt and Sanctuary... Какая же это нудятина. Серьёзно.
Первая часть мне очень понравилась. Стиль был довольно специфичным, и к нему нужно было просто привыкнуть. Дизайны врагов приятные глазу и интересны.
Вторая часть должна была взять всё самое лучшее из первой и приумножить, но...
Но вышло что-то другое.
Перед тем, как начать ругать игру, могу сказать: боёвка в ней хорошая; ресурсы для крафтов дают с запасом. В этом плане она очень даже справедлива.
В прохождении игры вам будут попадаться Боссы-маги, и это главная проблема игры (за исключением проблемного и раздражающего платформинга). Допустим вы нашли босса, убили его, и, казалось бы - идите дальше, но нет! Босс перерождается как мини-босс, начинает шататься по локации (в которых опять очень легко потеряться), и при контакте с ним, он легко может сбежать в другую часть. Допустим... Но таких может быть до пяти на уровне! И если вы собираетесь убить одного босса, путь к нему (а боссы-маги тоже любят убегать, пока мы не прижмём их к их арене) может проходить через другого босса.
А эти засранцы ещё любят призывать своих миньонов.
Буду честен, я не брезгую фармить ради оружки и брони, но не в принудительном же порядке!..
И это главный раздражающий фактор игры. Обычные, сюжетные боссы мне нравятся - с ними легко и приятно драться.
А теперь - платформинг. Сам по себе он нормальный, и никаких проблем нет. Они возникают только там, где у края платформ могут стоять враги (а они будут там стоять), и им как два пальца скинуть вас вниз. И это будет преследовать вас на протяжении всей игры. Буквально 30% смертей были оттого, что меня просто сбросил противник с выступа.
Игра в целом неплохая, но очень сильно начала раздражать первые... [b]15 часов[/b].
P.S. в сюжет вообще никак не вникал, поэтому упустил этот пункт.

I'll give a cautious recommendation. Firstly I enjoyed It enough to platinum it both on PS4 and PC that said its not without its issues. The Onlines pretty dead but the covenants are cool if you get lucky enough to encounter people their is also no lvl cap to assist or invade I've been invaded and assisted by lvl 999 players at lvl 31 and vice versa. The Game itself has a nice soundtrack, atmosphere and combat, Also unfortunately a lot of cheap gotcha moments with both traps and enemies. Getting Chain grabbed to death in Altarstone by the British there was not fun. The player model is way too easily ragdolled by a bunch of things despite poise/heavy armor which can be frustrating and the stamina and endurance systems aren't doing enough always felt like I'd run out of stamina even at 20+ will and equip load is honestly kinda ♥♥♥♥ lvl 40+ endurance will get you a midroll though if your not going super heavy with armor which is too much stat investment imo. The Mage fights are fun and can get outright chaotic and evil and They do fight eachother and also have unique minion designs for each mage though obviously some model and move reuse it does get tedious constantly chasing them though. Finally perfect block (you should still block occasionally) instead of parry just feels kinda awful give me back my salt and sanctuary shield system. The skilltree is large the weapons are fun the glyph (magic) system is you'll either love or hate it but I enjoyed playing more with them unlocked rather then not.

[h2]《如果你在尋找一款能與朋友一起體驗類魂風格的遊戲，這款絕對是你的最佳選擇》[/h2]

請把他當作一款合作遊戲就好，你會快樂許多。

首先，他與絕大多數[b]線上合作的類魂遊戲[/b]不同，他並非闖入其他玩家戰役幫忙，而是與其他玩家共同戰役一起冒險。

我很高興終於有這樣連線機制的類魂遊戲，我甚至認為每一款標榜線上合作的類魂遊戲都必須做到這一點。


[h2]《但這是一款褒貶不一的遊戲呀？》[/h2]

拋開那些想法，拋開那些前作忠實粉絲的評論。
相信我，以一款合作遊戲來說，你會感到快樂的。

我必須聲明我並非遊玩過前作《鹽與聖所》的玩家。
而我的朋友他甚至是一名類魂遊戲的初見人士，但我們體驗下來並沒有任何不適，甚至被這款遊戲絕佳的豐富度深深吸引。


[h2]《法師是這款遊戲的敗筆嗎？》[/h2]

在許多負評評論提到的，絕大部分都是裏頭的法師追逐機制很狗血，甚至被法師圍毆。

法師追逐戰我認為確實有點糟糕，但是被圍毆倒是不至於。

以下是法師的介紹，不算暴雷，可以跳過:

[spoiler]所謂的法師是遊戲裡一名瞬移型Boss，在開戰會一直在地圖瘋狂移動，而追趕他到某個位置時將會與他進行一戰，才能消滅他。

而消滅掉的法師在之後又會出現，所以很容易出現舊的法師與新的法師大量出現的狀況。

但是我從來沒有因為複數法師而被打回存檔點過，原因在於法師之間其實會互相殘殺的，可以看著他們廝殺再享獲漁翁之利，不要貿然行事捲入他們的鬥爭當中就可以有很好的體驗。[/spoiler]

被法師圍毆的狀況是很大機率可以迴避的，而且法師位置都有符號標示，很難會落入圍毆的戰局。[strike] (除非你腦衝)[/strike]

追逐戰在多人遊玩體驗很好，分開跑圖追法師實在輕鬆。


[h2]《遊戲豐富度》[/h2]

關於這一點是毋庸置疑的，光技能樹就非常龐大了，可以玩出自己所喜愛的流派。

遊戲武器更是豐富，雖然普攻幾乎大同小異但是特定武器有著不同的專屬技能，練起來是很有成就感的，無論是選擇什麼武器都有新穎的體驗。

地圖探索很有類銀河城的味道，每個地圖都相當廣大且設計有趣，是與朋友共同探索不會無聊的好遊戲。


[h2]《總結》[/h2]

很久沒有玩到這麼有趣的遊戲了，讓我回想起玩Oline遊戲的回憶，這款遊戲是非常有料的，過癮且鮮少重複性。

再加上我是中世紀黑暗風格的死迷，這款遊戲的美術我是真的太喜歡了。

所以為什麼評論是褒貶不一???
最后一张图败坏了所有的好感，恶心的小怪和地图，最终BOSS（伪）战还不如一个无名法师技能多。更蛋疼的是最终战前不给任何提示，打完直接结束强制进二周目了...这个二周目也是个奇葩，等级和装备是保留了，地图道具全部回收，难怪最后几个成就获取率腰斩式减少，谁tm要完整的从头玩一遍你这游戏

NS上已经150小时了，steam版买来支持一下制作组。游戏还行。你接受了法师换区，追逐法师这个玩法后就是爽玩。这个游戏其实是把怪猎刷大怪素材+类魂战斗+银河恶魔城的锁钥设计全部一锅炖一起了，你所感受到不自然的地方都是三个玩法融合后经过取舍的部分。

看steam上褒贬不一有点冤，其实一定程度上被玩了初版游戏的自媒体带节奏了。现在这版游戏至少值一个多半好评。游戏内容也对得起这个价钱。
The first one had such great atmosphere and map design. This one has not.

ボスが逃げるのやクエスト形式なのは構わないが、後半のボスの隙消し＆多段高速遠距離攻撃＆吹き飛ばし+多段攻撃の設置攻撃の全部盛りは、はっきり言って異常そしてただただ不快。

I'm writing this while currently replaying the game for a 3rd time, after a year of the game having been out or so, and getting it on steam after initially having it on Epic.
to be clear: I never had an issue personally with Ska taking epic's money, I fully support the decision, money is being given to a team I like a lot.
but
This is a game I feel nothing but contempt for.
I loved the designs, art direction, and vibe of Salt & Sanctuary, ever since I played it on the xbox as a kid.
I loved charlie & I loved vampire smile, primarily for the art, the style just felt so at home to me.
This game feels like it was made by a completely different artist, not just one that'd grown up, but one that'd changed fundamentally. From the colors, to the design of the enemies, even the bosses; everything feels- IS weaker.
I feel there are so many changes made in Sacrifice that almost come across as a slap to the face for someone that loved what made Sanctuary unique compared to other 2d metroidvania souls-likes. I can't even begin to understand why some changes were made at all. 
One that immediately stood out to me was the removal of parrying, in favor of perfect blocking, a much stingier and far less rewarding option. Parrying gave you a since of earned empowerment, a skill you'd hone with each enemy you fight, tagged with the reward of a finisher that gave you a massive lead in damage; this felt good. Perfect blocking, a significantly more time consuming and riskier option, would never be able to compete due to it's requirement being all the more demanding. Is there any reason this change needed to happen? All it's resulted in is some enemies, very few albeit, feeling mind numbing to fight due to their low attack cooldown. I suppose I could spend more time learning the perfect block timings on some of these enemies, but grueling through learning the timings and the amount of times I'd need to perfect block, just to do the damage I'd do in 2 hits anyways, is a little ridiculous? I felt similarly about elden rings approach to parrying, with the introduction of some bosses requiring multiple parries, albeit with that instance you are granted a moment to retaliate inbetween micro-parries.
I feel I could tangent on about the removal of parries for a while, I guess I just liked the feeling it gave a lot and miss it's existence in this game. Another issue I take with the game is it's bosses, not mages, the bosses you fight in every area (like 2 every area...?) which have no reward to speak of. I remember first fighting the spider with the glaive in the desert, and thinking I'd get her glaive that has red lightning infused magic and play around with it, only to beat her and be rewarded with nothing but a single upgrade material I didn't even need. Not a single boss in this game, not including the first boss you fight in the lake, has any kind of gear to be rewarded to the player? Also, that first boss in the lake, a significant downgrade to the sodden knight. 
On the topic of boss design, all of them are incredibly weak and seemingly thoughtless in their design it feels. Maybe it's meant to capture a specific mythological aesthetic, but it had gone over my head throughout my time with the game. Almost every boss in Sanctuary, for me, was incredibly memorable and fun to fight, even if not all of them had a reward to speak of. Their designs carried the experience and made the fights all worth it,  yet in Sacrifice it feels as if several bosses lack any kind of strong, imposing detail to them that makes them either memorable or interesting. Especially the final boss. The final boss, felt very heavily like it was a knock off version of the first games final boss, with a significantly worse design, worse music, and no where NEAR as amazing a set-up as the first games. Walking through the upside down salt city to the final boss in Sanctuary felt incredible, it felt like I'd entered a palace of impossibility, sending all sorts of feelings up and down my body when I'd first reached it. I loved this moment with the first game, this final moment. 
I can hardly remember the final bosses design, let alone the introduction to them, as I recall it feeling abrupt and out of the blue, like I'd just been dropped into the final bosses room while still thinking I was only going through another normal area with nothing special to it. This was beyond disappointing after all this time.
I just hopped in a call with my friend to play divinity OS2, and I can't bring myself to care to finish writing this.
I love Ska and their work, but this is a stain in my eyes that I'd rather forget about. 
It makes me happy to see them still updating Sanctuary after all this time, adding new features to it, and I can only hope this game will continue to receive updates, maybe even some massive changes brought in that bring the combat and feel of the game closer to the first some day? That's a bit of a pipe dream. 
Please keep working on new things Ska and don't let this game be the last one. I believe in your team still, I know you have it in you.

[h1]steam上最棒的可在线联机类银河恶魔城游戏[/h1]
只需要认清楚和前作盐与避难所不是一个赛道的游戏就可以愉快游玩了。
没打过后续免费更新的DLC地图“圣坛石祭坛”的牢玩家强烈建议回坑尝试一下，这个DLC地图设计密集结合了跳跳乐与4个很有挑战性和观赏性的非法师BOSS战诚意满满，美术也很好看，我认为是目前ska工作室的巅峰。

个人盐1PC+NS打了110+h，盐2epic首发第一时间3天打了全成就，后面也有和人联机合作/入侵（题外话能实装走私商这么猥琐的创新多人玩法简直天才，能不能逆输入到from本家的游戏里？ ），单说作为一个类银恶魔城+2D闯关动作游戏真的挺不错的，真的——配得上优秀二字！
但作为类魂是不太可以的，叙事水平退化，地图探索不停的被法师追逐打断节奏，就总体氛围感上来说是远不如前作的。或者说这作正是有意去掉这些要素而去添加了一些怪猎/仁王这些偏刷子和动作游戏，以及多人的要素。
看样子游戏是支持PC端跨平台联机的，所以其实epic买过的人也不用回到steam买[strike]我买来就当给ska奶粉钱了[/strike]，虽然应该不用特意提但在steam购买会继承epic存档。

关于大伙都最关心的盐与皮球，新版本给倒地和大部分受击状态加了一定无敌帧的同时加快了人物落地速度（指被弹飞后），现版本很难（就目前来说一次都没有）出现被怪无限连和皮球人的情况了。
恐石峰的雾霾羊头现在无法把玩家顶出半个地图距离了，简直感人。
但被一些高冲击力的招式打中还是会飞足够从一些狭窄平台罗伯特的距离，所以这点需要见仁见智...个鬼！谁喜欢这种东西！法师追逐更是老样子追杀（X）被遛狗（√），好在现在至少打的多确实会影响最终战血量而不是初版那样几乎不受影响。
可能大伙都说盐1的BOSS都太傻了于是2代就直接改成傻逼了：超高速抬手，人车复读，无限精戳戳戳应有尽有，后期数据爆炸动作已经够逆天了伤害超高根本抗不了几下，但总算还是能打过的（而且挺快的）所以就姑且当还是考虑了一些平衡吧。

游戏没有一代那种震撼的氛围是真的（二代我有这种感觉的地方的只有无垠天穹下的陨星号），剧情也细碎缺乏了一些像1代给人冲击力和思考空间的展开，但依旧是个各方面设计踏实的游戏。
盐1是个很保守的游戏，盐2让我看到了ska的野心以及再次认识到了他们有强大的制作功底。
地图虽然从1代的无缝大地图模式改为了类似恶魔之魂的拱心石关卡制但设计感与探索方面做得足（说个大家不爱听的，盐1有很多捷径可以说没有任何用处，现版本的盐2就算在加了篝火间传送的功能后依旧没有哪个捷径给我的感觉是无用的，这可以看作是地图设计进步的一个点吧？），人物动作底子比1代更优秀，画面升级，更加丰富的武器与装备系统以及[b]极其流畅稳定，现代化的，可以称之为完美的无缝联机多人体验-和好友直接从教程直接打到通关没有任何问题，全程大地图共享进度的同时中途不需要退出哪怕一次联机[/b]恐怕让这个总体评测褒贬不一的游戏成为了目前steam上最好的冒险岛/造梦西游[strike]但我觉得还是造梦更好玩[/strike]多人代餐，只要能拉个人过来一起吃史互相笑对方身上多脏还是说的上足够好玩的，单人就只能默默品史潸然泪下了...但还是挺好玩的！

虽然已经是过去的事情了这个游戏的联机体验不管是合作还是入侵都非常非常有趣且加入方式极其方便快速，特别是游戏刚开服人多+玩家们对游戏理解还没有悟透各有各的打法的时候，不同誓约不同目的的各玩家都在这么一个富有设计感的地图中厮杀，人一多起来欢乐和混乱程度直逼任斗。
现在风评影响导致的少人口和研究出的固定套路成型后根本匹配不到什么人，游戏一大乐趣与设计重点都无法体验了，只能说当时首发玩到这个游戏的多人模式的欢乐直接抵消了我对本作的各种负面情绪，希望有生之年本作也能迎来第二个游玩热潮吧！（会有吗？会有的！）

最最最后这个游戏里肢体动作有可互动的”拥抱“，杀完BOSS/入侵者后互相抱抱真的很温馨！


好怀念首发版游戏的暴风法师啊...联机看小伙伴被吹上天摔下来后又被吹上天摔下来然后再次被吹上天摔下来真的很快乐，有没有办法退版本还我一个纯真风法联机体验？

一直期待后面的地图会做的更精彩，恶心了10小时玩到第四张地图，我幡然醒悟：盐2真的没救了，不叫好也不叫座是有原因的。
直言不讳省流版：市场上有很多游戏可以选择，这个游戏仅在5块以下具有《收藏》价值，不建议游玩，很难理解明明有佳作经验的制作组怎么能做这样一个盐2来。
加入大量的法师狩猎战，弱化了剧情BOSS的存在，不仅仅是战斗体验上的赤石，更是对游戏剧情是一个极大的拖累，五六张地图大几十个“法师”，看上去内容十足，但从剧情角度出发这就是“法师们”，约等于红橙黄绿青蓝紫的史莱姆，再多史莱姆它还是史莱姆，游戏流程预估也只有20小时左右，已经玩了一半了，令我觉得感慨的并不仅仅是“我咋完全不懂剧情呢？”，更严重的是我对剧情没有丝毫的探索欲望，这样类魂的黑暗奇幻剧本明明是可以写的很精彩的，塞入了各色各样的史莱姆直接让剧本变得稀里糊涂起来。
糟糕的地图设计、无聊干瘪的剧情、要让你追到天涯海角的法师，我觉得盐2真是烂透了
过了这么久了，游戏优化得不错了，倒地保护也有了，至于新的地图模式和boss打法，我觉得很新颖，很有可取之处，作为盐1盐2都玩过的玩家，十分推荐本作，希望盐越做越好！
触发一个明显较短的结局后不能回到结局前的存档，没有询问直接进入二周目

[h1]プレイしてみて賛否両論なのも納得だなーと思った[/h1]

良い点
・スキルツリーと武器防具からなる個性の出しやすさ
　選択できる武器防具が多くて思わず迷っちゃう
　重い物、軽い物、リーチが長い物に魔法や爆発物、投擲などなど
　スキルツリーもかなり広い上に払い戻しでツリーを途中で切る事が出来る
　枝の途中にあるステータスノードだけを払い戻ししてさらにその先を伸ばせる
・オンライン要素
　ソウルシリーズにある協力の白、対戦侵入の赤、警察の青はある
　他にも複数あるみたい
　特定の人とだけやりたいならパスワード決めも可能

・ボスモンスターがとにかく逃げる逃げる

Having almost 100%'d the first game, I wanted to wait until I beat this game to give a review. I can now say that I've done so. TL;DR, Salt and Sacrifice is a devolution compared to its predecessor. While the core mechanics behind combat, boss fights, and metroidvania style exploration are the same, almost everything that was changed did so for the worse. I cannot recommend Salt and Sacrifice in good faith, when you instead have the option to play Salt and Sanctuary.

     1. Areas are less interconnected, as you travel to them through a central portal rather than finding them through exploration. Exploration within these areas is also typically rewarded with shortcuts that become obsolete due to the fact that you can teleport between beacons.
     2. Combat is less fluid and clunkier, especially with ranged weapons, which had their ability to combo and parry removed. Just holding them out will also make your character a sitting duck. Spells are now tied to weapons instead of being mixed and matched at user discretion, staves are mostly useless since almost all of their spells can be found on better weapon types. Despite the relatively complex movesets that each melee weapon has, I almost always did nothing but spam running attacks.
     3. There are fewer equipment options. Pistols no longer exist, axes have been scattered across multiple weapon types, and parrying and tower shields went the way of the dodo. The game places a heavy focus on having the right element to attack enemies, but not only does the bestiary not inform you of their weaknesses, many weapon categories also completely lack about half of the available damage types.
     4. The mages are the main enemy of the game, which you'll be fighting once about every 5-15 minutes, and which are required for both exploration and equipment progression. The mages also universally have the most frustrating designs and movesets in the game, which is especially baffling since the non-respawning bosses still have the same tight and exhilarating gameplay of the first game's bossfights.
     5. Almost the entire story is hastily crammed into the last area of the game, and told to you by a few nameless NPCs. The final boss is a nobody who you've never met or been told about before. The endings are devoid of a clear meaning. The NPC which gives you advice on progression throughout the game also mysteriously disappears at some point in the latter half and never comes back, which is acknowledged by no one.
     6. The sense of allegiance and progression that the different factions had in Sanctuary is gone. All factions in Sacrifice are built around a completely dead multiplayer, and the only other way to progress through them is to mindlessly grind even more mages, as if you hadn't been swamped enough by their presence.

I wanted to like this game. I *expected* to like this game but despite pushing through my initial dislike, I honestly grew to hate it. Everything about this game seems hostile to your enjoyment. So much so that I started to question if I was misremembering how much I enjoyed the first game. 

The level design is terrible. The combat is simplistic and bland. The levelling structure is weird and unsatisfying. The weapons are boring. The bosses are samey and limited in design. The main gameplay loop of chasing down said bosses as they teleport around the level is just a huge chore. The knockback and juggling on hit is such a bizarre instance of game design, it all just leaves me scratching my head. Poise of enemies seemed inconsistent and unpredictable and stamina was tuned in a way that killed the pace of fights. Maybe I could've steered my build in a way to mitigate these annoyances but honestly, the game didn't give me enough to ever care to try.

I'm fine with trying something new but not if it sucks every last drop of fun out of the game. What a disappointment.
